sources.description: Modal GPU Glossary — 73 precise technical definitions across 4 categories
NVIDIA CUDA Programming Guide — 5 parts, 20 chapters, full TOC extracted
CUDA C++ Best Practices Guide — 21 chapters
Hopper GPU Tuning Guide — TMA, Thread Block Clusters, HBM3, NVLink4
Ampere GPU Tuning Guide — async copy, arrive/wait barrier, TF32, HBM2e
cuBLAS API Reference — GEMM, batched GEMM, tensor core math modes
CUDA Math API — intrinsic math functions
Nsight Compute HTML — NOT PRESENT locally; referenced via llms.txt only
PTX ISA Reference — NOT PRESENT locally; referenced via llms.txt only
NVIDIA CUDA Samples — 115 samples across 5 categories
GPU Mode Lectures — 79 lectures, code in lecture_001/003/004/008/009/012/018
CUTLASS 4.4.1 — README, core abstractions, CuTe DSL, architecture support
llm.c GPT-2 CUDA training — 25 CUDA kernel files in dev/cuda/
FlashAttention 1/2/3/4 — README, hopper headers, FA4 CuTeDSL architecture
FlashAttention paper (Dao et al. 2022) — 4094 lines extracted via pdftotext
sources.notes: Extracted: IO complexity theorem (Θ(N²d²M⁻¹)), Algorithm 1 tiling, block sizes (Bc=4M/d, Br=min(4M/d,d)), recomputation backward pass, optimality lower bound, benchmark speedups (7.6× GPT-2, 15% BERT MLPerf)

sources.description: FlashAttention-2 paper (Dao, 2023) — extracted via pdftotext
sources.notes: Extracted: sequence-length parallelism (§3.2), warp work partitioning split-Q vs split-K (§3.3), causal masking block skipping 1.7-1.8× speedup (§3.1.1), non-matmul FLOP reduction, logsumexp L unification, 225 TFLOPs/s training benchmark

sources.description: FlashAttention-3 paper (Shah et al., 2024) — extracted via pdftotext
sources.notes: Extracted: WGMMA warp-specialization producer/consumer (§3.1), 2-stage GEMM-softmax pipeline Algorithm 2 (§3.2), FP8 layout conformance + in-kernel transpose (§3.3), block quantization, incoherent processing, 740 TFLOPs/s FP16 / 1.2 PFLOPs/s FP8 on H100

sources.description: Megatron-LM paper (Shoeybi et al., 2019) — extracted via pdftotext
sources.notes: Extracted: column/row parallel linear layers (§3), f/g conjugate operators (§3 Code 1), attention head parallelism, 4 all-reduces per transformer layer, 76% scaling efficiency 512 GPUs / 8.3B parameters

sources.description: ZeRO paper (Rajbhandari et al., 2020) — extracted via pdftotext
sources.notes: Extracted: Stage 1/2/3 memory equations (§5), communication volume analysis (§7), 16Ψ mixed-precision Adam memory breakdown, Table 1 per-device memory consumption, super-linear speedup 64-400 GPUs

sources.description: Programming Massively Parallel Processors (Kirk & Hwu) — 12 chapters extracted via pdftotext
sources.notes: Chapters extracted: Ch1 Introduction, Ch2 GPU History, Ch3 CUDA (data parallelism, CGMA ratio, matrix multiply), Ch4 CUDA Threads (warp execution, exercises 4.2/4.3 on tiling correctness), Ch5 Memories (tiled matmul, CGMA, exercises 5.1-5.4), Ch6 Performance (branch divergence, occupancy, bandwidth, exercises 6.6-6.9), Ch8 MRI case study, Ch9 Molecular Visualization (coalescing), Ch10 Problem Decomposition, Ch11 OpenCL, Ch12 Conclusion

sources.description: CUDA by Example (Sanders & Kandrot) — 12 chapters + appendix extracted via pdftotext
sources.notes: Chapters extracted: Ch1 Why CUDA, Ch2 Getting Started, Ch3 CUDA C (kernel launch, passing params, device query), Ch4 Parallel Programming (vector add, fun example), Ch5 Thread Cooperation (shared memory, dot product, synchronization), Ch6 Constant Memory + Events (ray tracer, cudaEventElapsedTime), Ch7 Texture Memory (heat transfer simulation), Ch8 Graphics Interop, Ch9 Atomics (histogram), Ch10 Streams (page-locked memory, CUDA streams, multi-stream), Ch11 Multiple GPUs (zero-copy), Ch12 Final Countdown (cuFFT, cuBLAS, profiling tools), Appendix A Advanced Atomics (hash table)

ingestionNotes: Phase A addendum (2026-03-16): 7 PDF files successfully ingested via pdftotext (poppler-utils). All 5 papers and 2 books now marked complete.
FlashAttention 1/2/3 papers: precise IO complexity theorems, algorithm pseudocode, Hopper-specific optimizations (TMA, WGMMA, FP8) extracted and added to cluster-llm-kernels.json as 9 new concepts.
Megatron-LM paper: column/row parallel linear layers and f/g conjugate operators extracted; new tensor-parallelism topic added to cluster-llm-kernels.json.
ZeRO paper: Stage 1/2/3 memory equations and communication analysis extracted; new distributed-memory-optimization topic added to cluster-llm-kernels.json.

PMPP (Kirk & Hwu, 12 chapters): CGMA ratio concept, data parallelism concept, PMPP citations added to existing memory/execution/algorithms/linear-algebra/profiling concepts; 2 new exercises added (naive matrix multiply, tiled matmul with CGMA analysis).
CUDA by Example (Sanders & Kandrot, 12 chapters + appendix): constant memory exercise, kernel timing with CUDA Events exercise added; citations added to foundations and memory clusters.
Nsight Compute HTML not present locally (only referenced in llms.txt). Nsight Compute section relies on GPU Mode Lecture 1/8 code and gpu-glossary/perf entries.
PTX ISA HTML not present locally. PTX concepts derived from gpu-glossary/device-software/parallel-thread-execution.md and gpu-glossary/host-software/nvcc.md.
CUTLASS docs/ directory contains generated HTML Doxygen — structure too dense for direct extraction; CUTLASS concepts extracted from README.md which is comprehensive.
All 73 GPU Glossary entries successfully read and used to populate concept definitions with precise technical language and verified key facts.
llm.c dev/cuda/ directory contains 25 CUDA kernel files providing real production patterns for: attention, matmul, softmax, layernorm, gelu, adamw, crossentropy, encoder, nccl all-reduce.
FlashAttention FA4 CLAUDE.md provided detailed architecture documentation for the most recent (CuTeDSL) implementation including SM100 2CTA patterns.
cuda-mode-lectures README documents 79 lectures. Only lecture directories with code files (001, 003, 004, 008, 009, 012, 018, 029, 036, 057) were directly useful.
